sections.paragraph:
τόπος, ου, ὁ [etym. complex]

—1. ‘a spatial area’

—a. anonymous geographical area(s) place Mt 14:35; 24:7; Mk 13:8; Lk 4:37; 10:32; Ac 12:17; 27:2; 1 Th 1:8; Rv 20:11; with notation about condition Mt 12:43; 14:13; Mk 1:35; 6:35; Lk 16:28; J 6:10; 2 Pt 1:19.

—b. named locality Mt 27:33; Mk 15:22; Ac 27:8; Rv 16:16.

—c. location for some object, activity, condition, or phenomenon: place, space Mt 26:52 (a sheath); 28:6; Mk 6:11; Lk 2:7; 14:9f; 19:5; J 4:20; 11:48 (temple); 14:2; 18:2; 20:25 v.l.; Ac 1:25b (in wordplay); 6:13f (temple); 1 Cor 14:16; Hb 8:7; Rv 2:5; 6:14. Of a passage in a book Lk 4:17; cp. Ro 9:26.

—2. ‘a position with obligation’, responsibility Ac 1:25a.

—3. ‘a circumstance that offers a chance to do someth.’, opportunity Ac 25:16; Ro 15:23; Eph 4:27; Hb 12:17; of a change in jurisdiction by giving God the chance Ro 12:19.
